evidence_scope: source-model authority for earned storage-intent acknowledgment receipts, policy predicates, caller-visible result projection, and refusal boundaries
runtime_boundary: model evidence only; this does not validate mounted runtime behavior, QEMU crash or fault behavior, fsync or fdatasync barrier semantics, distributed transport execution, successor or comparator wording, production readiness, or release readiness

reviewed_authorities.role: normative storage-intent policy and receipt boundary
caller-visible result, refusal, evidence-cut, retry, and retention proof boundary
current receipt identity and placement receipt projection boundary
claim registry and blocked evidence requirement authority

receipt_authority_laws.statement: Every successful storage-intent acknowledgment must carry a named guarantee receipt.
A success reply is legal only when earned evidence satisfies the requested policy predicate.
Placement, transport, caching, relocation, and rebake choices implement policy; they do not define or weaken the requested acknowledgment floor.
RAM, cache, remote-memory, or weaker authority can satisfy only an explicit volatile or preview class whose loss boundary and downgrade projection are visible to the caller.
Missing, stale, contradictory, unauthorized, redacted, compacted, unsupported, or incomplete evidence must project a typed refusal or degraded-visible result, not generic success.
POSIX sync, FUA, and barrier-style success require a configured stable-storage floor with flush, fence, replay, and persistence evidence for the named scope.

must_refuse_when: flush or persistence domain evidence is absent
the earned class is weaker than requested
proof needed by the caller is redacted or compacted away
membership or fence evidence is stale
peer receipts do not match the requested policy

required_authority: explicit unsafe or preview policy admission
must_refuse_when: the surface would project the answer as durable success

evidence_query_rules.statement: Evidence queries used for acknowledgment receipts must be bounded snapshots that are complete for the requested policy purpose.
When evidence cannot be collected or exposed at the needed cut, the result must name a typed refusal instead of inferring success from partial evidence.
Unbounded live scans, cache-local guesses, dashboard samples, and mixed-policy bundles are not receipt authority.

result_refusal_rules.required_projection: refused or blocked with membership, quorum, and fencing refs
unknown-evidence or retry-after-fresh-evidence with stale family refs
refused or unsafe-visible with media capability refs and no strengthened receipt
degraded-visible or refused result naming requested and earned classes
blocked or refused with retention and recall proof gap refs

non_claims: This artifact does not validate mounted runtime receipt production.
This artifact does not validate QEMU crash or fault behavior.
This artifact does not validate POSIX fsync, fdatasync, FUA, or barrier semantics.
This artifact does not validate successor, comparator, production, or release-readiness wording.
